title_en: Birth and Childhood of Muhammad ﷺ
content_en:
Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib was born in Mecca, in the Year of the Elephant — approximately 570 CE — so named because in that year Abraha al-Ashram, the Abyssinian viceroy of Yemen, marched against Mecca with a war elephant at the head of his army, intending to destroy the Kaaba. His campaign was miraculously thwarted, as described in Surah al-Fil: 'Did He not make their plot go astray? And He sent against them birds in flocks, striking them with stones of hard clay, making them like eaten straw.' The dramatic providential character of the year of the Prophet's ﷺ birth was not lost on subsequent generations of Muslims.

Muhammad ﷺ was born into the clan of Banu Hashim, of the tribe of Quraysh — the custodians of the Kaaba and the most prestigious tribe of Arabia. His father, Abdullah, died before his birth or very shortly thereafter, and his mother, Aminah bint Wahb, would die when he was approximately six years old. He was thus an orphan from early childhood — a reality that the Quran itself references in a verse of profound personal tenderness: 'Did He not find you an orphan and give you refuge? And find you lost and guide you? And find you poor and enrich you?' (Quran 93:6-8).

According to the Arabian custom of the time, the infant Muhammad ﷺ was entrusted to a wet nurse from the desert tribes, so that he might be raised in the clean air of the countryside and acquire the pure classical Arabic of the Bedouin. His wet nurse was Halimah al-Sa'diyyah of the Banu Sa'd tribe. The time with Halimah is remembered fondly in Islamic tradition and is the setting for one of the earliest extraordinary events associated with the Prophet's life — the opening of his chest (sharh al-sadr) in which two angels came to him, opened his chest, removed a portion of his heart, washed it with Zamzam water, and returned it. This narration is recorded in Sahih Muslim and is understood by scholars as a spiritual purification in preparation for the prophetic mission.

After his mother's death, Muhammad ﷺ was raised by his grandfather Abd al-Muttalib, the revered chief of Quraysh, for approximately two years until Abd al-Muttalib also passed away. He was then entrusted to his uncle Abu Talib, who raised him with genuine affection and would remain his protector and defender until Abu Talib's own death forty years later, when the Prophet ﷺ was fifty years old.

From his youth, the Prophet ﷺ was known among his community by the title 'al-Amin' — the Trustworthy. His character was distinguished by honesty, discretion, and an innate recoiling from the moral errors of his society. He never worshipped idols, never consumed alcohol, and never participated in the licentiousness common among young men of his social station. As a young man he tended flocks — an occupation shared by most of the prophets before him, as he himself noted — and made commercial journeys with his uncle Abu Talib to Syria. These early experiences exposed him to the diversity of the world, the trade routes that connected civilizations, and the traditions of Ahl al-Kitab (People of the Book), all of which formed part of the providential preparation for his later mission.
title_id: Kelahiran dan Masa Kecil Nabi Muhammad ﷺ
content_id:
Muhammad bin Abdullah bin Abdul Muthallib dilahirkan di Makkah pada Tahun Gajah — sekitar 570 M — yang dinamakan demikian karena pada tahun itu Abrahah al-Asyram, wakil raja Abyssinia di Yaman, berbaris menuju Makkah dengan gajah perang di kepasukan pasukannya, berniat menghancurkan Ka'bah. Kampanyenya digagalkan secara ajaib, sebagaimana dikisahkan dalam Surah Al-Fil dalam Al-Qur'an.

Ayah beliau, Abdullah, meninggal dunia sebelum Muhammad ﷺ dilahirkan — atau tak lama setelah kelahirannya. Ibu beliau, Aminah binti Wahb, meninggal dunia ketika Muhammad ﷺ baru berusia enam tahun, dalam perjalanan pulang dari Madinah di tempat bernama Al-Abwa'. Jadilah beliau seorang yatim piatu di usia sangat dini.

Muhammad ﷺ kemudian diasuh oleh kakeknya Abdul Muthallib — tokoh yang paling dihormati di Makkah — yang sangat mencintainya. Namun kakeknya pun meninggal ketika beliau baru berusia delapan tahun, meninggalkan pengasuhan beliau kepada pamannya Abu Thalib.

Masa menyusu: Sesuai tradisi Arab, Muhammad ﷺ diserahkan untuk disusui kepada Halimah binti Abu Dzu'aib dari suku Sa'd, di pedalaman Jazirah Arabia. Selama berada bersama Halimah, keluarga tersebut mengalami keberkahan yang luar biasa. Al-Mubarakpuri mencatat peristiwa pembelahan dada (syaqqus shadr) yang terjadi ketika beliau masih kecil — malaikat membelah dada beliau dan membersihkan hatinya. Setelah itu, Halimah mengembalikan beliau kepada ibunya karena khawatir.

Tanda-tanda kenabian terlihat sejak masa kecil: kejujurannya yang luar biasa, akhlaknya yang mulia, keengganannya untuk berpartisipasi dalam perbuatan yang tidak bermoral, dan kebijaksanaannya yang melampaui usianya. Bangsa Arab menjulukinya Al-Amin — orang yang dapat dipercaya.
